content:
Define the TopModule module declaration with the exact required ports and widths: clk, reset, in_valid, in[7:0], out_valid, and out[7:0]. Declare output ports as logic so they can be driven by sequential logic.

Retrieved Related Information:
Info 1: clk: input logic, 1-bit (Type: Signal)
Info 2: in_valid: input logic, 1-bit (Type: Signal)
Info 3: in: input logic [7:0], [7:0] (Type: Signal)
Info 4: out_valid: output logic, 1-bit (Type: Signal)
Info 5: out: output logic [7:0], [7:0] (Type: Signal)
Info 6: reset is active-high synchronous and clears all output registers. (Type: StateTransition)
source:
Implement a module named TopModule with the following interface:
- input  logic       clk
- input  logic       reset
- input  logic       in_valid
- input  logic [7:0] in
- output logic       out_valid
- output logic [7:0] out

[Strict Interface Contract]
Your generated TopModule must keep the exact port names and widths below.
Do not rename ports. Do not add/remove ports. Do not change bit-widths.
- clk: 1-bit
- in: [7:0]
- in_valid: 1-bit
- out: [7:0]
- out_valid: 1-bit
- reset: 1-bit

content:
Implement the out_valid output register as positive-edge-triggered synchronous sequential logic. On reset, set out_valid to 0. Otherwise, assign out_valid from in_valid each cycle, creating the required 1-cycle valid pipeline.

Retrieved Related Information:
Info 1: reset: input logic, 1-bit, active-high synchronous and clears all output registers.  
Info 2: in_valid: input logic, 1-bit.  
Info 3: out_valid: output logic, 1-bit.  
Info 4: reset is active-high synchronous and clears all output registers.  
Info 5: When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1.  
Info 6: When in_valid is 0 at cycle N, out_valid must be 0 at cycle N+1.  
Info 7: Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
source:
Behavior requirements:
1. Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
2. When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out must equal (in + 8'd1) from cycle N.
3. When in_valid is 0 at cycle N, out_valid must be 0 at cycle N+1.
4. reset is active-high synchronous and clears all output registers.

content:
Implement the out output register as positive-edge-triggered synchronous sequential logic. On reset, set out to 8'd0. Otherwise, when in_valid is 1, register the incremented input value in + 8'd1 into out so it appears one cycle after the accepted input. When in_valid is 0, drive a deterministic registered value such as 8'd0 because out_valid will be 0 on the corresponding output cycle.

Retrieved Related Information:
Info 1: reset: input logic, 1-bit, active-high synchronous and clears all output registers.
Info 2: in_valid: input logic, 1-bit.
Info 3: in: input logic [7:0].
Info 4: out: output logic [7:0].
Info 5: out_valid: output logic, 1-bit.
Info 6: reset is active-high synchronous and clears all output registers.
Info 7: When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out must equal (in + 8'd1) from cycle N.
Info 8: When in_valid is 0 at cycle N, out_valid must be 0 at cycle N+1.
Info 9: Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
source:
Behavior requirements:
1. Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
2. When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out must equal (in + 8'd1) from cycle N.
3. When in_valid is 0 at cycle N, out_valid must be 0 at cycle N+1.
4. reset is active-high synchronous and clears all output registers.

content: Keep the implementation self-contained in TopModule only, with synthesizable RTL and no instantiated reference modules or additional benchmark modules.
source:
Implementation constraints:
- Keep the module synthesizable.
- Use only TopModule in this single-file benchmark.
- Do not instantiate RefModule.
- Keep exact port names and widths.
